raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "State legislation allowing mobile pharmacies to dispense medications used to treat opioid addiction has reached a critical milestone in the legislative process.",
    "AB 663 will help local efforts to address the opioid crisis, prevent overdose deaths, and further improve access to healthcare for the city’s most vulnerable patients.",
    "Opioid use disorder is often defined by withdrawal symptoms, intense cravings, and risky behaviors that can put one’s health and safety at risk.",
    "Two FDA-approved medications for the treatment of opioid use disorder, buprenorphine and naltrexone, can be prescribed in outpatient clinics and dispensed by retail pharmacies.",
    "Despite improved access to medications for opioid use disorder, significant barriers to treatment continue to exist.",
    "One challenge that clinical outreach teams face is providing medications for opioid use disorder in locations accessible to people experiencing homelessness.",
    "AB 663 allows local jurisdictions to choose to operate a mobile pharmacy that dispenses medications for opioid use disorder to clients with a valid prescription.",
    "Expanding access to life-saving care through mobile pharmacies will help reduce overdose deaths for individuals who otherwise would be unable to access the needed resources.",
    "AB 663 has the potential to save lives by making life-saving medications for opioid use disorder more accessible.",
    "California law permits pharmacies to operate mobile units as extensions of their brick-and-mortar pharmacy licenses.",
    "These mobile units cannot carry or dispense controlled substances, including buprenorphine, which is one of the most effective treatments for opioid use disorder.",
    "AB 663 addresses the gap in legislation by allowing mobile pharmacies to carry Schedule III, Schedule IV, or Schedule V controlled substances approved for the treatment of opioid use disorder.",
    "Mobile pharmacies providing medications to treat opioid use disorder will complement other public health interventions aimed at addressing unintentional overdose deaths.",
    "Expanding access to medications for opioid use disorder is part of the City’s Overdose Prevention Plan, which aims to reduce drug-related deaths."
  ]
}
```
